channel.title: IBM on My Personal Blog
channel.description: Recent content in IBM on My Personal Blog

item.title: Custom Paperweight - HPC Heat Sinks
item.description:
1st official release of this paperweight prototype made 18 months ago for a special occasion (my colleagues at Exaion will recognize it)

After several trials and failures, the final version seems quite successful (many thanks to Emmanuel RIVET for his patience and the quality of his engraving work)

No laser engraving on copper and a CNC-machined QR code is not readable by a smartphone. Hence the mix of both.

The raw material? It seemed nicer to me to reuse these heat sinks from out-of-service HPC blades (IBM iDataPlex) rather than putting the motherboard in WEEE waste and the heat sink in the scrap bin (it would certainly never have reached the recycling center…).

item.title: ISEQ Logo: Art from Recycled IBM Components
item.description:
Well, it’s finally been revealed during the 7th annual meeting of ISEQ Pôle d’excellence de l’Industrie des Systèmes Électroniques du Québec. This logo was ordered many months ago, following the inauguration of a site in Sherbrooke last year, where my first logos were unveiled.

The IBM Connection

Due to the proximity (and not just geographically) with IBM, Alexandra Tolza asked me to maximize IBM used parts to create that logo. This challenge pushed me to source authentic IBM components, transforming obsolete technology into a meaningful piece of art that represents the electronic systems industry in Quebec.
